rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 28, 2019, Line 11, Plaintiff Ticketfly, LLC's Motion For Terminating Sanctions. Plaintiff Ticketfly, LLC's motion for terminating sanctions against Defendants Parish Entertainment Group, Jason Perkins, and Michael O'Connor is granted, and Plaintiff is awarded monetary sanctions against Defendants and their counsel Francis M. Defendants' answer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint, filed July 19, 2018, is stricken, and Defendants' default shall be entered. (Code Civ. Proc. Secs. 2023.030(d), 2023.010.) (The remainder of the Court's tentative ruling for this motion has been e-mailed to counsel for parties.) rulings.Case Title: MEDLEY CAPITAL CORPORATION VS. SECURITY NATIONAL GUARANTY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses And Responsive Documents From Plaintiff Medley Capital Corporation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 27, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANTS SECURITY NATIONAL GUARANTY, INC., EDMOND GHANDOUR'S Motion To Compel Further Responses And Responsive Documents From Plaintiff Medley Capital Corporation Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to khuibonhoa@chjllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EMILY ALAMANO VS. ROYLAND HOLMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Supp Discovery (Def. Royland Holman)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 27, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT ROYLAND HOLMAN'S Motion To Compel Supp Discovery (Def. Royland Holman) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part. Court considered both the late opposition and the late reply, in addition to the moving papers. Motion to compel denied as moot because Plaintiff served verified responses to Defendant's supplemental form interrogatories (set one) on January 28, 2019, prior to the filing of the instant motion on January 29, 2019. Request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $360. Plaintiff states that in a January 26, 2019 email, Defendant granted an extension to serve the discovery responses to January 28, 2019, and that Plaintiff complied with that deadline. The referenced January 26 email between counsel, which Plaintiff does not provide with her opposition, is attached as Exhibit G to the Declaration of Ryan Kraft. It states in relevant part: "I [Defendant] will be filing a motion to compel on Tuesday [January 29, 2019]. I will be requesting sanctions. If you deliver verified responses after I file the motion, I'm still going to ask for sanctions for having to file the motion. You may serve verified responses to our office on Monday [January 28, 2019] if you wish to avoid the motion." To avoid the instant motion, Plaintiff should have acted in a manner that was calculated to ensure that Defendant received the discovery responses on January 28 or at least received notice by that date that the responses were being served, so as to avoid filing the instant motion on January 29. Plaintiff does not offer any explanation as to why she served the discovery responses by mail on January 28, and did not alert Defendant that she was doing so. Plaintiff also does not provide any explanation as to why the discovery response were over four months late in the first place. Under these circumstances, the Court finds that some sanctions are warranted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to khuibonhoa@chjllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
